message:
feat(ftui): migrate Triage view with ranked items, workflow panel, and action affordances (FTUI-05.5)

Adds full Triage view to the ftui backend:
- Vertical layout: triage list (severity/section/title), workflow progress
  panel (expandable), and details/actions panel
- Key handling: j/k/Up/Down navigation, Enter/a primary action, 1-9
  numbered actions, m mute, e toggle workflow expand
- Digit routing guard prevents global view switching in Triage view
- Data refresh with triage items and active workflows via QueryClient
- 18 new tests (navigation, actions, mute, workflow expand, rendering)
- 83 total ftui_stub tests passing

Includes scaffolding by LavenderGrove (fields, key handler, imports,
refresh_data integration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1m2","title":"Explanation templates: reusable reason patterns for wa why and errors","description":"# Explanation Templates: Reusable reason patterns for wa why

## Purpose
Provide consistent, helpful explanations for common scenarios via template system.

## Implementation

### Template Structure
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExplanationTemplate {
    pub id: &'static str,
    pub scenario: &'static str,
    pub brief: &'static str,
    pub detailed: &'static str,
    pub suggestions: Vec<&'static str>,
    pub see_also: Vec<&'static str>,
}
```

### Built-in Templates

#### Policy Denials
```rust
pub static DENY_ALT_SCREEN: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.alt_screen",
    scenario: "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    brief: "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    detailed: r#"
The pane is currently displaying an alternate screen buffer, which typically
means a full-screen application like vim, less, htop, or similar is running.

Sending text while alt-screen is active could:
- Corrupt the application state
- Cause unintended keystrokes
- Interfere with user interaction

The safety policy blocks sends to alt-screen panes by default.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Exit the full-screen application first",
        "Use --force if you're certain this is safe",
        "Configure policy to allow specific alt-screen apps",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"],
};

pub static DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.command_running",
    scenario: "Send denied because a command is running",
    brief: "Another command is currently executing in the pane",
    detailed: r#"
The pane has an active command running (detected via OSC 133 markers or
heuristics). Sending text while a command runs could:

- Interrupt the running command
- Queue input for later (confusing)
- Cause the shell to misinterpret input

wa waits for command completion before sending unless overridden.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Wait for the current command to finish",
        "Use Ctrl-C to cancel the running command first",
        "Use --wait-for to send after a specific pattern",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa status", "wa send --wait-for"],
};

pub static DENY_RECENT_GAP: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.recent_gap",
    scenario: "Send denied due to recent output gap",
    brief: "Pane had no output recently, possibly waiting for input",
    detailed: r#"
wa detected a gap in pane output that suggests the pane might be:
- Waiting for user input at a prompt
- Displaying a confirmation dialog
- In an unknown state

The policy requires a prompt marker (OSC 133) or manual confirmation.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Check the pane manually to see its state",
        "Use --force if you've verified the pane is ready",
        "Enable OSC 133 support in your shell for better detection",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa capabilities --pane <id>"],
};
```

#### Workflow Explanations
```rust
pub static WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "workflow.usage_limit",
    scenario: "Why handle_usage_limits workflow was triggered",
    brief: "Codex hit its daily token usage limit",
    detailed: r#"
The Codex agent reported it has reached its usage limit. This typically
happens when:

- Daily token quota exceeded
- Account-level rate limiting triggered

The handle_usage_limits workflow will:
1. Gracefully exit the current Codex session
2. Parse the session summary for resume ID
3. Select an alternate OpenAI account
4. Complete device auth flow
5. Resume the session with new credentials
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Let the workflow complete automatically",
        "Check account status with: caut status",
        "Configure account pool in wa.toml",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa workflow status", "caut"],
};
```

### Template Registry
```rust
lazy_static! {
    pub static ref EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES: HashMap<&'static str, &'static ExplanationTemplate> = {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();
        m.insert("deny.alt_screen", &DENY_ALT_SCREEN);
        m.insert("deny.command_running", &DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING);
        m.insert("deny.recent_gap", &DENY_RECENT_GAP);
        m.insert("workflow.usage_limit", &WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT);
        // ... more templates
        m
    };
}

pub fn get_explanation(id: &str) -> Option<&'static ExplanationTemplate> {
    EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES.get(id).copied()
}
```

### Template Interpolation
```rust
pub fn render_explanation(
    template: &ExplanationTemplate,
    context: &HashMap<String, String>,
) -> String {
    let mut output = template.detailed.to_string();
    for (key, value) in context {
        output = output.replace(&format!("{{{}}}", key), value);
    }
    output
}
```

## Testing
- All templates have valid structure
- Interpolation works correctly
- No broken cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Templates for all common denial reasons
- [ ] Templates for all workflow triggers
- [ ] wa why uses templates consistently
- [ ] Templates documented in help

 {"id":"wa-1pe","title":"[EPIC] Dry-Run Preview Mode for All Human Commands","description":"# [EPIC] Dry-Run Preview Mode

## Mission
Allow users to **preview any action before executing** it. This builds confidence, enables learning, and prevents mistakes.

## Why This Is Essential
Users are scared to run automation commands because:
- "What if I send to the wrong pane?"
- "What if the workflow does something unexpected?"
- "What if I misconfigured something?"

Dry-run mode eliminates this fear:
- See exactly what would happen
- Verify target resolution is correct
- Understand policy evaluations
- Learn wa's behavior safely

## Scope
Add `--dry-run` flag to all state-modifying human commands:
- `wa send --dry-run`
- `wa workflow run --dry-run`
- `wa approve --dry-run`
- `wa setup --dry-run` (already partially supported)

## How It Works

### wa send --dry-run
```bash
$ wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "Reread AGENTS.md"

DRY RUN - No changes will be made

Target Resolution:
  Pane: 3 (claude_code @ /home/user/project)
  Domain: local

Policy Evaluation:
  ✓ Rate limit: 2/10 sends in last minute (within budget)
  ✓ Pane state: PromptActive (safe to send)
  ✓ No recent gaps (continuity OK)
  ✓ Command safety: text appears safe

Expected Action:
  - Inject 18 characters via wezterm cli send-text --pane-id 3
  - Wait for: prompt boundary (timeout: 30s)

To execute for real:
  wa send --pane 3 "Reread AGENTS.md"
```

### wa workflow run --dry-run
```bash
$ wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 7 --dry-run

DRY RUN - No changes will be made

Workflow: handle_compaction
Target: Pane 7 (codex @ /home/user/project)

Pre-checks:
  ✓ Compaction anchor found in pane tail
  ✓ Pane not in AltScreen
  ✓ No recent output gaps
  ✓ Policy: workflow execution allowed

Steps that would execute:
  1. Acquire workflow lock for pane 7
  2. Stabilize: wait for tail stability (no new deltas for N polls; max 2s)
  3. Send: "Please re-read AGENTS.md...\n"
  4. Verify: wait for prompt boundary
  5. Mark event handled

Estimated completion: Steps 1-5

To execute for real:
  wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 7
```

## Implementation Strategy
1. **DryRun context flag**: Thread `dry_run: bool` through command execution
2. **Policy evaluation without action**: PolicyEngine already separates evaluation from execution
3. **Step plan generation**: Workflow engine generates steps without executing
4. **Consistent output format**: DryRunReport struct for all commands

## Key Design Decisions
- Dry-run output is **structured** (can be JSON for robots)
- Dry-run is **fast** (no waiting for actual execution)
- Dry-run **does not acquire locks** (safe to run in parallel)
- Dry-run **validates inputs** (catches errors early)

## Testing
- Unit tests: dry-run produces expected output for known inputs
- Integration tests: dry-run never modifies state
- Contract tests: dry-run output matches actual execution path

## Success Criteria
- All human commands that modify state support --dry-run
- Dry-run output clearly shows what would happen
- Dry-run never modifies any state (DB, panes, locks)
- Robot mode commands also support dry-run (JSON output)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All state-modifying human commands support --dry-run.
- Dry-run never mutates DB, locks, or panes.
- Dry-run output is deterministic in TTY and JSON modes.
- wa-1pe.5 tests pass.

 {"id":"wa-1pe.1","title":"DryRunContext infrastructure: thread dry_run flag through command execution","description":"
# DryRunContext Infrastructure

## Purpose
Create the foundational infrastructure to thread dry_run mode through all command execution paths.

## Implementation

### DryRunContext Struct
```rust
pub struct DryRunContext {
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub report: DryRunReport,
}

pub struct DryRunReport {
    pub command: String,
    pub target_resolution: TargetResolution,
    pub policy_evaluation: PolicyEvaluation,
    pub expected_actions: Vec<PlannedAction>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}
```

### Threading Pattern
All command handlers receive `CommandContext` which includes `dry_run: bool`.
When dry_run is true:
1. Execute all validation and resolution
2. Build DryRunReport instead of executing
3. Return report as structured output

### CLI Integration
```
$ wa send --dry-run "text"
$ wa workflow run --dry-run handle_compaction
$ wa approve --dry-run <code>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DryRunContext and DryRunReport structs defined
- [ ] CommandContext carries dry_run flag from CLI
- [ ] Helper functions for building report sections
- [ ] JSON output for robot mode
- [ ] Human-readable output for TTY

## Testing
- Unit tests for dry_run flag propagation and no-side-effect guards.
- Integration tests: verify no DB/lock/pane mutation under dry-run.
- E2E: add a dry-run scenario with verbose logs and artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-37x.5","title":"Graceful degradation modes: continue operating with reduced functionality","description":"
# Graceful Degradation Modes

## Purpose
When components fail, continue operating with reduced functionality rather than crashing.

## Degradation Scenarios

### 1. DB Read-Only Mode
**Trigger**: DB write failures (corruption, disk full)
**Behavior**:
- Continue observing panes
- Queue writes in memory (bounded)
- Log warning about degraded mode
- Resume normal operation when DB recovers

### 2. Pattern Engine Failure
**Trigger**: Pattern compilation error, regex timeout
**Behavior**:
- Skip pattern detection
- Continue ingesting output
- Log which patterns are disabled
- Partial detection if some patterns work

### 3. Workflow Engine Pause
**Trigger**: Workflow step fails repeatedly
**Behavior**:
- Pause the failing workflow
- Continue other workflows
- Keep observing for new events
- Notify user of paused workflow

### 4. WezTerm Unavailable
**Trigger**: CLI consistently fails
**Behavior**:
- Stop action attempts
- Continue any cached observation
- Poll for WezTerm availability
- Resume automatically when available

## Status Reporting
```
$ wa status

System Status: DEGRADED

Active Degradations:
  ⚠ DB writes queued (disk full) - 47 pending
  ⚠ Workflow paused: handle_usage_limits (step 3 failed)

Core Functions:
  ✓ Observation: active
  ✓ Detection: active
  ✗ Actions: degraded
```

## Recovery
- Automatic: Poll for recovery conditions
- Manual: `wa recover <component>` to retry

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each degradation mode implemented
- [ ] Status reports degraded state clearly
- [ ] Automatic recovery when conditions clear
- [ ] Manual recovery command works
- [ ] Core observation continues in all modes

## Testing
- Unit tests for breaker/backoff logic and state transitions.
- Integration tests simulating IO failures and recovery paths.
- Chaos harness scenarios with verbose logs and recovery assertions.

 {"id":"wa-38vw","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-05 Screen-by-Screen View Migration","description":"## Purpose
Migrate all wa TUI screens/views to ftui widgets/runtime without losing operator functionality.

## Why
Screen parity is the user-visible definition of migration success.

## Focus
- navigation shell
- home/panes/events/triage/history/search/help migrations
- per-view parity matrices and behavior acceptance criteria","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:17.849837202Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:26:18.880261223Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","migration","views"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-38vw","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:57.267645490Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-38vw","depends_on_id":"wa-1ssn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:59.579669050Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-38vw","depends_on_id":"wa-2wed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:17.875728911Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}],"comments":[{"id":272,"issue_id":"wa-38vw","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"### Execution Rationale (FTUI-05)
FTUI-05 performs screen migration in a controlled order (shell -> core views -> support views) with parity ledgers per surface.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:26:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3akp","title":"FTUI-05.4.a Events view annotation/label/triage visibility parity checklist","description":"## Background
Events view drives operator incident response; migration must preserve annotation/label/triage visibility semantics.

## Deliverables
- parity checklist for feed ordering, annotation context, labels, triage state, and redaction-sensitive fields
- validation scenarios for high-volume event streams and mixed-severity events
- artifact mapping to snapshots, adapter fixtures, and E2E transcripts

## Acceptance Criteria
- event semantics match legacy expectations or are documented as intentional deltas
- redaction behavior is explicitly verified and logged
- outputs include deterministic evidence usable in CI and post-failure diagnosis.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:21.138965449Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:43.580388678Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","subtask","views"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3akp","depends_on_id":"wa-23bz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:21.158113462Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3akp","depends_on_id":"wa-2i6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:09.608641325Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
-{"id":"wa-3any","title":"FTUI-05.7 Migrate Search + Help views and small-screen behavior","description":"## Background\nSearch/help are high-usage support surfaces and regression-prone on narrow terminals.\n\n## Deliverables\n- search result rendering + query UX in ftui\n- help/reference view migration\n- small-terminal layout resilience checks\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- search/help parity confirmed by checklist\n- rendering remains stable on constrained dimensions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:21.817126373Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:31:02.326469887Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","search","views"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3any","depends_on_id":"wa-1hbj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:19:10.296995301Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3any","depends_on_id":"wa-38vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:21.852380388Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3any","depends_on_id":"wa-sx5v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:19:10.401486123Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-3cso","title":"FTUI-03.2 Enforce one-writer output routing for UI and log surfaces","description":"## Background\nfrankentui requires a one-writer rule. wa must eliminate direct stdout/stderr writes from active UI runtime paths.\n\n## Deliverables\n- unified output sink routing UI redraw + logs\n- explicit policy for subprocess output capture/routing\n- violation detection strategy in tests/CI\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- no direct terminal writes bypass output gate in migrated paths\n- sustained output does not corrupt cursor/layout state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:51.521424086Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:44:58.629048059Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:44:58.628915122Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","one-writer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3cso","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:51.534554022Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3cso","depends_on_id":"wa-2qyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:24.231283380Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3fed","title":"FTUI-03.4.a Add panic/abort teardown harness and restoration assertions","description":"## Background
Panic/abort paths must restore terminal usability and preserve forensic context.

## Deliverables
- teardown harness that simulates panic/abort and validates restoration invariants
- assertions for cursor mode, alt-screen state, and output routing cleanup
- failure artifact capture requirements (logs, transcripts, environment markers)

## Acceptance Criteria
- harness reproduces abrupt-exit scenarios deterministically
- restoration invariants are verified and regressions fail loudly in CI
- produced artifacts are sufficient for post-mortem without rerunning interactively.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:11.354154882Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:33.838982036Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","lifecycle","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3fed","depends_on_id":"wa-1p3f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:11.370228861Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3fed","depends_on_id":"wa-3gsu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:45.362884125Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3ftn","title":"FTUI-06.3 Implement modal interactions for approvals/confirmations/errors","description":"## Background\nSafety-critical flows need explicit modal UX semantics.\n\n## Deliverables\n- reusable modal patterns (confirm/error/info) in ftui\n- keyboard-first interaction model\n- integration into existing approval/mute/critical-action flows\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- modal interactions are consistent and accessible\n- unsafe actions require explicit confirmation paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:28.506264195Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:19:49.770714606Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","modal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3ftn","depends_on_id":"wa-2h1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:19:49.770658412Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3ftn","depends_on_id":"wa-2zd7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:28.515943600Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
